backfill_source_binding: The immutable source identity is committed after this self-contained source commit and before acquisition; every receipt embeds the exact source commit and protocol hash.
provider_time_policy: Fetch and retain provider server-time evidence for every bounded kline request; observed availability is the maximum of response-complete UTC, HTTP Date, and associated provider server time.
raw_response_retention: Exact response bytes are SHA-256 bound and retained immutably with deterministic gzip compression; verification decompresses before hashing.
normalized_fragment_policy: Closed-schema records include event time, provider close time, current observed availability, current acquisition time, and receipt identity; canonical bytes are SHA-256 bound and retained with deterministic gzip compression.
receipt_chain_policy: Separate append-only R1P5 ledger beginning at the all-zero SHA-256; durable entries bind immutable receipt/raw/fragment hashes and actual durable completion time.
daily_partition_policy: UTC symbol/day; a normal complete day requires exactly the 1,440 expected distinct monotonically increasing minute opens plus complete receipt, raw, fragment, provider-time, and clock evidence.
checkpoint_policy: Immutable generation-named checkpoints bind the P4 activation and live chain terminal, R1P5 chain terminal, sorted daily partition identities/hashes, authority hashes, exposure ledger, evaluation-cutoff floor, and exact scope; mutable aliases are rejected.
conflict_and_duplicate_policy: Byte-equivalent logical candle overlap is idempotent; any differing content at one symbol/minute is CONFLICT and fails closed; no silent last-write-wins.
live_backfill_concurrency: Backfill uses a separate root, lock, cursor namespace, receipt chain, and binary; it never takes or mutates the P4 live collector lock, state, receipts, or cursors.
readiness_policy: Structural readiness requires at least 180 consecutive all-symbol UNEXPOSED_PIT_EVIDENCE_COMPLETE UTC days and every frozen structural condition; candidate code and candidate counts are forbidden.
candidate_research_prohibition: Do not execute, calculate, inspect, reveal, or summarize candidate signals, events, exclusions, clusters, outcomes, returns, performance, parameters, research partitions, validation, or holdout state; do not create real RIF state.
